text: If you're a spec wearer you'll know there are times when glasses are just not practical.
Like on the footy field or when you're cooking or when you're about to go for a run. A lot of
people have put off trying contact lenses because they're afraid to touch their eye,
but if you insert them properly they should feel as natural as your own eye. In fact once they're
in you won't feel like you've got them in at all. The hard part about putting lenses in is
overcoming your blink reflex. The eyes are designed to protect themselves from airborne
dust particles and flying objects like tennis balls or your brother's elbow. For women it can
be a lot easier trying contact lenses in for the first time because they may have been wearing
mascara or eye makeup and so they've already learned to control their blink reflex. If you're
interested in trying contact lenses it's worth getting your optometrist
to help you for the first time so that you can experience them without having to worry about
putting them in. If you've decided to start wearing them regularly follow these simple
tips to make sure they stay comfortable and your eyes stay healthy. Always wash your hands
thoroughly before touching your eyes or your lenses. Bacteria on your hands can cause serious
infections. If you drop a lens wash it thoroughly with contact lens cleaning solution or discard it
and start with a fresh one. It can cause serious infections if you drop a lens wash it thoroughly
with contact lens cleaning solution or discard it and start with a fresh one. It can cause serious

infections if you drop a lens wash it thoroughly with contact lens cleaning solution or discard it. It can be hard to stop the blinking if you try and put the lens directly on your eye. To make it easier trick your eye looking off to the side and then pop the lens onto the white of the eye and slide it onto your iris. Use your finger to hold your eyelid out of the way. Never sleep in lenses that are not designed for overnight wear as the lenses may dry out and you could injure your eye when you remove it. Always follow the cleaning and care directions specified for the type of contact lenses. If you have any concerns about your eyes, please contact us.
Whenever you have eyes, ask your local Specsabers optometrist during your regular eye test.
Even if you don't wear glasses, it's important to have your eyes tested every two years to
make sure they are healthy and everything is in working order.
